PayoutRequest.form.confirm: I agree to transfer the funds from your current balance to your registered bank account. The amount will be included in the next payout.
PayoutRequest.form.title: Available funds to payout to your account
aboutSectionHeroContent: Gitpay connects payments to real deliverables. Issues can be funded with bounties, pull requests can unlock rewards, and service providers can request payments for work delivered through repositories and tracked files. The result is a more transparent and reliable way to fund and pay for digital work.
aboutSectionHeroContent2: Building a collaborative digital economy where delivered work is verifiable and payments are connected to real outcomes.
aboutSectionHeroContent3: The principles that guide how we build, collaborate, and move money through work-centered flows.
aboutSectionHeroItem10Secondary: Every task completed and every payout processed helps teams deliver faster with accountability and trust.
aboutSectionHeroItem11Secondary: We handle every payment, agreement, and collaboration with reliability and integrity, ensuring confidence for all sides of the ecosystem.
aboutSectionHeroItem3Primary: Funders and clients see measurable delivery from every payment.
aboutSectionHeroItem3Secondary: Every funded task is linked to tracked work, helping teams verify progress before payout.
aboutSectionHeroItem4Primary: Work is funded with clear expectations.
aboutSectionHeroItem4Secondary: Define scope, attach funding, and connect payouts to what needs to be delivered.
aboutSectionHeroItem5Primary: Teams can organize delivery through repositories.
aboutSectionHeroItem5Secondary: Import and track issues, coordinate contributors and service providers, and keep execution tied to versioned work.
aboutSectionHeroItem6Primary: Contributors and specialists are rewarded for delivered results.
aboutSectionHeroItem6Secondary: From pull requests to scoped services, completed work can be verified and paid with transparent rules.
aboutSectionHeroItem7Secondary: We believe in clear collaboration and communication. Tasks, funding, and payouts stay visible to participants with the right context.
aboutSectionHeroItem8Secondary: Everyone involved — teams, contributors, service providers, and funders — gets clear terms for participation, delivery, and payment.
aboutSectionHeroItem9Secondary: Gitpay is built for collaborative execution across repositories, teams, and independent professionals working toward shared outcomes.

bountiesPublicPage.bestFit.content: Gitpay works well for developers who want flexible paid work, visible delivery, and payouts connected to accepted code.
bountiesPublicPage.bestFit.item.secondar: Browse bounties and pick issues aligned with your skills and availability.
Your delivery stays connected to commits, pull requests, and review history.
Turn merged contributions into payouts through Gitpay.
The reward is tied to accepted work, so expectations stay visible from the start.
bountiesPublicPage.bestFit.title: Why Gitpay for contributors?
bountiesPublicPage.callToAction.title: Start earning from delivered work on Gitpay
bountiesPublicPage.content: Browse funded issues, deliver solutions through pull requests, and get paid when your work is accepted. Gitpay gives contributors a clearer path from picked task to payout.
bountiesPublicPage.title: Earn from Work on Gitpay
charge.customers.page.bestFit.content: Gitpay works well for service providers who sell services or digital deliverables.
charge.customers.page.bestFit.item.secon: Keep a clear record of payments and delivery.
Send a simple payment link to your clients.
Track payments and payouts in one place.
Gitpay is designed for service providers delivering digital services.
charge.customers.page.bestFit.title: Why Gitpay for service providers?
charge.customers.page.callToAction.title: Start receiving payments for delivered services on Gitpay
charge.customers.page.content: Get paid for your work requests to your customers deliveries in one place. Gitpay's Service Payments streamline the process of requesting, delivering, and receiving payments for services directly through our platform.
charge.customers.page.title: Charge Customers on Gitpay

fundWorkPublicPage.bestFit.content: Gitpay works well when you want funding attached directly to concrete tasks and payouts tied to accepted delivery.
fundWorkPublicPage.bestFit.item.secondar: Use bounties to help maintainers move priority issues forward.
Make the reward visible before work starts so contributors know what is being funded.
Keep payouts connected to the pull request and merge flow your team already uses.
A good fit for maintainers, sponsors, and teams coordinating work through Git.
fundWorkPublicPage.bestFit.title: Why Gitpay for funding work?
fundWorkPublicPage.callToAction.title: Start funding work with clear delivery rules on Gitpay
fundWorkPublicPage.content: Attach a bounty to an issue, invite contributors to deliver through pull requests, and release payment when the work is merged. Gitpay helps teams and sponsors move from funded backlog to verified delivery with a clear record of what was paid for.
fundWorkPublicPage.title: Fund Work on Gitpay

welcome.cta.hero.title: Start getting paid or fund work today.
welcome.hero.main.description: Fund issues, reward merged pull requests, or get paid directly. Gitpay gives you a transparent flow to manage work and payments end to end.
welcome.hero.main.title: The payment platform for work delivered.
welcome.pricing.contributors.description: We support direct transfer for your bank account registered on Gitpay for credit card payments or invoice
We support Paypal to receive payments when the bounty is paid using Paypal
8% fee to withdraw your bounty after the Pull request is merged

⚠️ Note: You will receive the payouts according to the payment method used by the maintainer, if the maintainer paid with credit card, you will receive the payout in your bank account, if the maintainer paid with Paypal, you will receive the payout in your Paypal account
welcome.pricing.contributors.subheader: For contributors who solve issues, you will have a fee to receive the transfer
welcome.pricing.contributors.title: Fee for contributors
welcome.pricing.maintainers.general.subt: The platform fee when you pay for a bounty on Gitpay
welcome.pricing.maintainers.title: Our pricing model
welcome.pricing.opensource.description1: Public projects from Github or Bitbucket
welcome.pricing.opensource.description2: We will match your issues with our contributor skills to find the right candidate
welcome.pricing.opensource.description3: 8% fee for payment with Credit Card or Paypal
welcome.pricing.opensource.title: Open source
welcome.pricing.private.description1: Private projects on Github or Bitbucket
welcome.pricing.private.description2: We will match your issues with our contributor skills to find the right candidate
welcome.pricing.private.description3: 18% fee for payment in Credit Card or Paypal
welcome.pricing.private.title: Private
welcome.pricing.serviceproviders.descrip: 8% to receive payouts automatically when your customer pay the payment request for your service
Receive payouts directly in your bank account
Charge customer with payment request links created on the platform
welcome.pricing.serviceproviders.subhead: For service providers who deliver services through Gitpay
welcome.pricing.serviceproviders.title: Fee for service providers
welcome.secondary.hero.item0.secondary: Send a simple payment link and get paid by clients worldwide — no setup required on their side and no back-and-forth about how to pay.
welcome.secondary.hero.item1.secondary: Fund issues, reward merged pull requests, and ensure payouts follow actual delivery — not promises or manual tracking.
welcome.secondary.hero.item2.secondary: Connects payments directly to the work being done.
welcome.secondary.hero.item3.primary: One flow for all your work
welcome.secondary.hero.item3.secondary: From open source contributions to private projects and client payments, manage everything in one place with a clear, transparent flow.
welcome.usecases.bounties.description: Get paid for work delivered on Git repositories.

welcome.usecases.bounties.item2: Submit pull requests and get your work accepted.
welcome.usecases.bounties.item3: Claim the bounty and receive payment through Gitpay in your bank account.

welcome.usecases.payment.item2: Setup payout schedules and get daily, weekly or monthly payouts directly on your account.
